title: Some analogies between arithmetic and topology
speaker: Tony Feng
abstract: There are striking analogies between topology and arithmetic algebraic geometry, which studies the behavior of solutions to polynomial equations in arithmetic rings. One expression of these analogies is through the theory of etale cohomology, which is an algebraic version of topological cohomology. I will give an introduction to this circle of ideas and illustrate how they played the role in the recent resolution of a 1966 conjecture of Tate concerning a certain duality in the etale cohomology of 2-dimensional varieties over finite fields.

proof_stages.label: Finite-field point counts as cohomological traces
proof_stages.summary: By comparing complex solutions of a genus-g curve with finite-field solutions and Frobenius fixed points, the talk motivates étale cohomology as the object whose traces compute point counts just as the Lefschetz formula computes fixed points.

proof_stages.label: Field of definition as topological base
proof_stages.summary: The base field is reinterpreted topologically: Spec Fpbar behaves like a point, Spec Fp like a circle with finite covers, so a variety over Fp resembles a fibration over S1 and a surface over Fp resembles a 5-manifold.

proof_stages.label: Brauer groups and linking pairings
proof_stages.summary: The Brauer group of such a surface, viewed through torsion in étale H3 up to the Tate-conjectural finiteness issue, is placed in analogy with middle torsion homology of an odd-dimensional manifold, and the arithmetic pairing is defined by the same formalism as the topological linking form.

proof_stages.label: Tate alternation conjecture for the arithmetic pairing
proof_stages.summary: The central question is whether the skew-symmetric arithmetic linking form is actually alternating, as conjectured by Tate, which would force the Brauer group order to be a square; after reviewing earlier evidence and corrections, the speaker states his theorem proving alternation for p not equal to 2.

proof_stages.label: Topological alternation criterion via Stiefel-Whitney classes
proof_stages.summary: A topological theorem relates alternation of the linking form on a smooth odd-dimensional manifold to the integral lift, equivalently Bockstein-vanishing, of the second Stiefel-Whitney class of the tangent bundle.

proof_stages.label: Steenrod and Wu operations remove smoothness
proof_stages.summary: Steenrod squares and Wu classes define the relevant Stiefel-Whitney classes purely topologically and in étale cohomology, thereby removing the smoothness hypothesis and giving an étale version of the alternation criterion.

proof_stages.label: Arithmetic verification from the fibration-over-circle picture
proof_stages.summary: For a surface over Fp, the topological picture is not an arbitrary 5-manifold but a fibration over a circle with complex-surface fibers, whose Chern-class lifts and trivial base tangent class force the criterion and hence the arithmetic pairing to be alternating.

examples.name: Genus-g hyperelliptic curve over C and finite fields
examples.note: The concrete genus-g curve makes visible that the 2g-dimensional middle cohomology seen over C reappears in Frobenius trace formulas for point counts over finite fields, producing the motivating analogy.
examples.engagement_reason: he analyzes the hyperelliptic curve over C by identifying its genus-dependent topology and cohomology dimensions, then over finite fields relates point counts to Frobenius traces
